So there is a book written by
Rupa Goswami, who is one of the six Goswamis of Vrindavan.
The name of the book is Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu, which in English we call it Nectar of Devotion.
So in that book, Rupa Goswami talks about six things which will destroy
Bhakti, Shadbhir Bhaktir Vinashyati.
Vinashyati means to destroy.
So what are those specific six things which we as practitioners also should be careful of,
so that there is no impediment in our path to spiritual advancement.
Many times we might wonder,
I have been chanting for so many years,
I still have so many lusty desires,
it appears that I have not advanced.
It appears that still I am far far away from what is being mentioned
as a symptom of a spiritually advanced person.
One may wonder, where am I going wrong?
I am trying to chant my rounds, I get up in the morning,
I visit the temple regularly, I attend the Gita classes,
I am doing my best.
Still, why am I not advancing?
The example is given just like,
if we are filling water in a bucket,
one thing is yes, we have to keep the tap open,
the water inflow is there in the bucket,
then for sure the bucket will get filled up.
We also have to do one very important thing
while we are filling the water in the bucket,
and that is to ensure the water is not leaking from the bucket.
Imagine a bucket in which there are big holes,
now in line with the topic what we are discussing today,
there are six big holes,
and we have the tap on, the water is getting filled,
but when you look at the bucket,
practically there is very little water which has got accumulated.
You may wonder, the tap is on,
water is flowing inside the bucket,
but why the bucket is not getting filled?
It is not enough that water has to go in the bucket,
it is also important that there is no leakage.
If there are big holes,
then practically all the water will get drained out.
So what are the big holes,
which will drain out all the advancement what we are making,
or rather it will retard our advancement.
Let's say if a person is walking,
he takes four steps forward and three steps back,
he takes two steps forward and three steps back,
eventually a person will not make a tangible progress towards his destination.
Likewise we see pendulum,
it keeps moving,
it is moving,
it is quite active,
there is lot of activity going on,

there is no net net progress.

as per the laws of motion.

the first,

The first principle,
the first thing which destroys bhakti is,
atya ahara.
Ahara means,
ahar also means food.
Atya ahara means,
atya means excess.
Here it not only refers to food,
no doubt over eating is not good,
not only material,
not only spiritually,
even materially.
We should resort to eating simple foodstuff,
sattvic foodstuff.
Krishna in the Gita says,
one who is regulated in his eating,
sleeping,
recreation and work,
can mitigate all material pains,
arising from this material contact.
The soul is in the material body,
it's a material contact.
Krishna also says,
one who eats too much,
or eats too little,
one who sleeps too much,
or does not sleep enough,
cannot be a yogi.
So atya ahara means,
when we eat too much,
something which is very palatable,
some dishes made,
it is very tasty,

predominantly to satisfy our taste buds.

eat to live,

hot,

the urges of our tongue,

eat all kinds of abominable things,

then this will destroy Bhakti.

collecting too much.

simple living,
high thinking.

we require money,
we require some,
near and dear ones,
some relationships.

to maintain those relationships,

collect too much,
hoard too much,
and not,
practice the principle of simple living,
and high thinking,
we can be violating this principle,
and it may endanger,
our spiritual advancement.
Atyahara.

when,

Because we are not getting it, we keep one goal after the other, one goal after the other,

The third principle which Rupa Goswami says which will destroy our bhakti is Prajalpa.
Prajalpa means talking unnecessarily.
Loose talk, frivolous talk, talking unnecessarily on some mundane subject matter.
Talking and discussing on some socio-political issues unnecessarily.
It's not in my circle of influence.
It may be in my circle of concern but predominantly I am spending lot of time discussing issues
which are in my circle of concern but I am not able to do anything about it.
Covey talks about the circle of influence and circle of concern in that Seven Habits
book.
So it is a search and discovery.
Many times people are engaged in unnecessary discussion, wasting their precious time.
As spiritualists, when we take up commitment to chant our rounds, read Bhagavad Geeta, hear
Bhagavatam, time is.
Everyone has only 24 hours in a day.
And at times devotees might wonder, you know, I am not getting time for my chanting.
I am not getting time to read.
read, quite possible the time is getting leaked, as I mentioned the water is falling in the
bucket but there are big holes and the water is leaking.
So we should be little cautious, careful, discern where my time is going.
There was one article which says that if we were to take an average of lifespan of a human
being, let's say 65 years or 70 years or 80 years, practically 22 years in those 65 years,
one third of our life, we sleep.

we end up sleeping.

the best part is that
by surrendering to the Lord,
by chanting the names of the Lord,
by depending on the Lord,
Mak Chitta Sarva Durgaani
Mat Prasad Attharishyasi,
Krishna says to Arjuna,
if you become conscious of Me,
Mak Chitta Sarva Durgaani,
Durgaani means all the difficulties,
all the challenges,
Mat Prasad Attharishyasi,
you will cross over all obstacles by My grace.
Pralad Maharaj encountered so many difficulties,
so many challenges,
he was put in a pit with snakes,
he was thrown from a mountain top,
he was put in a place where
wild elephants were,
you know, let loose.
They could goad him to death,
but then he was completely dependent on the Lord
and the Lord saved him.
So rather than losing hope,
we can intensify our dependence on the Lord,
we can chant more rounds,
chant attentively.
The problem is not
how my problems are not getting solved,
the problem is how I can
depend on the Lord,
how I can chant better.
If we can take care of that,
the Lord will take care of our problems.
This question is from Pratik Gupta,
Prabhuji, I am reading Bhagavad Gita
and also chanting for last one month,
but my family and friends keeps changing
their mind by saying,
read other Bhagavad Gita's
and follow this Guru, follow that.
I get confused, please help.
I can very confidently say
that Shri Prabhupada is a very bona fide Guru,
a pure devotee of the Lord,
a self-realized soul,
a pure spiritual master.
His Bhagavad Gita is very potent,
it is very evident all over the world.
Millions of people after reading
Shri Prabhupada's Bhagavad Gita
have become devotees of the Lord.
Krishna in the Gita says,
Man Mana Bhav Mad Bhakto,
towards the end in the 18th chapter,
think of me,
become my devotee,
worship me,
offer obeisances to me,
Madhyaachi Mam Namaskaru.
There are more than 700 editions
of Bhagavad Gita in the market.
People read it for some peace of mind,
but there is no transformation of heart.
They don't become Krishna conscious
in that sense.
They still have a very materialistic
perspective to life,
but they are pious and religious.
So I would strongly recommend
that yes, people will say so.
They may propose different solutions,
different books,
but first we should be convinced.
If we are convinced,
then yes,
we can not get swayed by such
proposals or arguments.
So this is definitely a very potent book.
Hare Krishna Maha Mantra is a potent process.
If we definitely stick on to that,
for sure we will advance.
So we will stop here today.
